news.title: World's biggest football stadium built to fight Bernabéu for World Cup final
news.lede: Spain and Morocco are fighting for the right to host the 2030 World Cup final. Morocco is building the biggest football stadium on the planet in the hopes of landing the World Cup final. The 2030 edition is being held across six countries in three continents, 100 years after the iconic tournament made its debut. Real Madrid‘s renovated Santiago Bernabéu, which cost $1.91 billion, is the favorite to land the showpiece.

news.title: ‘Everyone is hooked on football’: Indian league seeks to ride World Cup wave
news.lede: Club chiefs hope that a revitalised setup can help huge interest in this summer’s tournament translate into growth of the Indian domestic game. India was not at the World Cup, but the tournament drew huge interest from within the country. This has happened before but now comes with significant, and necessary, changes in the domestic game that could transform a quadrennial passion into perennial interest. After more than a year of negative headlines regarding the Indian Super League, the country’s top tier will kick off on 4 September.

news.title: Petition to replay Copa final takes off in 🇦🇷, tops 80,000 signatures
news.lede: The decision of the 2026 FIFA World Cup is still causing fallout off the pitch... in Argentina! A petition on the signature-collecting site change.org already has more than 80,000 signatures, “demanding” that the final against Spain be played again! Yes, you read that right: replay the final! The fans’ main “justification” is that the result of the match was allegedly “manipulated” by a “corrupt” referee.

news.title: US World Cup fans hit with brutal soccer reality after howler for Lionel Messi-less Inter Miami on MLS return
news.lede: With plenty of eyeballs on the domestic product as U.S. Soccer looks to build on a hugely successful World Cup, the game didn't get off to the best start. Miami defender Ian Fray rolled the ball to goalkeeper Rocco Ríos Novo in the 18th minute and he somehow managed to take a wild swing with his right boot, miss completely and see the ball trickle into his own goal. Thankfully for Fray, the effort went down as an own-goal for the goalkeeper to protect his stat sheet.

news.title: Megan Rapinoe blames Donald Trump for USA's World Cup demise
news.lede: Donald Trump’s intervention threw the USA ‘off their game’ at the World Cup (Picture: The Daily Show) Former USA footballer Megan Rapinoe says Donald Trump threw the men’s team ‘off their game’ and gave Belgium extra motivation to knock the co-hosts out of this summer’s World Cup. Trump inserted himself at the centre of the USA’s World Cup story after Folarin Balogun was shown a red card for a foul on Tarik Muharemovic in the side’s group-stage victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina. The sending off meant the Monaco striker, formerly of Arsenal, had been due to miss the USA’s all-important round-of-16 tie with Belgium through suspension. However, Balogun’s one-match ban was subsequently suspended for a year by FIFA, a decision which left the Belgian Football Association ‘astonished’ and ‘investigating potential options’.

news.title: FA can bring World Cup home in 2038 and trump USA to end 60 years of inexplicable England hurt
news.lede: How can England not host the World Cup – and not even be involved in the worldwide conversation? Those thoughts have increasingly rattled around my head in recent months, as a Houston yankee living and working in King Charles' court. They reached peak clamor when the United States publicly demanded first dibs on the next available World Cup (2038), after receiving mostly ringing praise from international players and fans during the record-setting success of the 2026 tournament. "We will be requesting it again immediately,” US President Donald Trump told FOX Sports.

news.title: From wonderkid to world champion: The tournament that changed Pau Cubarsi’s place in football
news.lede: There has always been an extra phrase attached to Pau Cubarsi. Intelligent, ‘for his age’. One of the best defenders in Europe, certainly, ‘for his age’. He was admired for his qualities, but, like a dog kept on a leash, his birth certificate was always used as an asterisk when describing him.

news.title: Dispatch: Fact-checking the World Cup final's fallout
news.lede: When Spain overcame Argentina after a tense final to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Sunday evening, it capped off six weeks of fierce competition that was accompanied online by plenty of commentary, claims and politics. Months before the tournament even began, football’s governing body came under intense scrutiny for its ticket prices and its awarding of a new peace prize to U.S. As the tournament got underway, much of the focus returned to the football. But all the way to the end, the match-ups were being linked to geopolitical narratives while fake, AI-generated content continued to proliferate.
